tool_name: Base Plate and Anchor Bolt Design Calculator - AISC EN 1993
category: Structural Engineering / Steel Connections / Base Plate and Anchor Bolt Design
standards: AISC Design Guide 1 (Base Plate and Anchor Rod Design)
ASTM A354 (Alloy Steel Bolts Gr. BC/BD)
calculation_basis: Steel column base plate and anchor bolt design screening using EN 1993-1-8 (design of joints), EN 1992-4 (fastenings in concrete), AISC Design Guide 1 (Base Plate and Anchor Rod Design), AISC 360-22 J9 (anchor rods and embedments), and ACI 318-19 Chapter 17 (anchoring to concrete). Design checks include: concrete bearing stress distribution under the base plate (tripartite linear distribution per Thornton / AISC DG1), required base plate area, base plate thickness by yield line cantilever method (AISC DG1 Eq. 3.1-3.2), anchor bolt net tensile area, steel strength of anchor bolts in tension, shear resistance (threads included or excluded), combined tension+shear interaction (AISC 360 J3.7 / ACI 318 17.5), concrete breakout strength in tension (ACI 318 17.6 / EN 1992-4 §7.2.1.4), concrete pullout strength (ACI 318 17.7 / EN 1992-4 §7.2.1.5), concrete side-face blowout (ACI 318 17.7.3, deep embedment ca1 > 0.4×hef), concrete pryout strength in shear (ACI 318 17.7.3 / EN 1992-4 §7.2.2.5), supplementary reinforcement effect (tension and shear), seismic anchor ductility requirements (ACI 318 17.2.3 / EN 1992-4 §2.2.2), anchor edge distance and spacing limitations, minimum embedment depth requirements (ACI 318 17.3.1.3), anchor group interaction (tension and shear), and governing overall connection utilization ratio. The output is a preliminary engineering screening calculation and must not replace sealed structural design, project-specific geotechnical report, national annex, detailed calculation, anchor installation inspection report, pull-out testing, or authority approval.

inputs.label: Design Code Selection
inputs.description: Select governing design code for base plate and anchor bolt design
options: Combined AISC + ACI 318 (preferred for US projects)

inputs.label: EN National Annex (if EN selected)
inputs.description: Select applicable National Annex for EN 1992-4 fastening design partial factors

inputs.label: Column Profile Type
inputs.description: Cross-section profile of the steel column supported by the base plate

inputs.label: Column Depth (d_c)
inputs.description: Overall depth of the steel column section parallel to the strong axis

inputs.label: Column Flange Width (b_fc)
inputs.description: Flange width of the steel column section

inputs.label: Column Flange Thickness (t_fc)
inputs.description: Flange thickness of the steel column

inputs.label: Column Web Thickness (t_wc)
inputs.description: Web thickness of the steel column

inputs.label: Column Steel Grade
inputs.description: Steel grade of the column
options: ASTM A572 Gr. 50 (Fy=345 MPa, Fu=450 MPa)
ASTM A500 Gr. C HSS (Fy=345 MPa, Fu=427 MPa)

inputs.label: Base Plate Width (N)
inputs.description: Width of the steel base plate (dimension perpendicular to column web, parallel to flange face). Typically N = bf_c + 2 × (edge distance).

inputs.label: Base Plate Depth / Length (B)
inputs.description: Depth (length) of the steel base plate (dimension parallel to column web). Typically B = d_c + 2 × (edge distance).

inputs.label: Base Plate Thickness (t_p)
inputs.description: Thickness of the steel base plate. To be checked vs. required thickness.

inputs.label: Base Plate Steel Grade
inputs.description: Steel grade of the base plate material

inputs.label: Base Plate Yield Strength (F_y,BP)
inputs.description: Nominal yield strength of the base plate material

inputs.label: Grout Compressive Strength (f_g)
inputs.description: Nominal compressive strength of the bedding grout (typically 50–80 MPa for non-shrink cementitious grout)

inputs.label: Grout Thickness (t_g)
inputs.description: Thickness of the grout layer between base plate and concrete foundation

inputs.label: Concrete Compressive Strength (f'_c)
inputs.description: Specified compressive strength of concrete foundation (cylinder strength for ACI / EN standards)

inputs.label: Concrete Foundation Plan Dimension (parallel to N)
inputs.description: Plan dimension of the concrete support surface parallel to base plate width N. Used for bearing area magnification factor sqrt(A2/A1).

inputs.label: Concrete Foundation Plan Dimension (parallel to B)
inputs.description: Plan dimension of the concrete support surface parallel to base plate depth B.

inputs.label: Anchor Bolt Diameter (d_b)
inputs.description: Nominal diameter of the anchor bolts. Common sizes: M12–M64 per ISO 898, or 1/2 in to 4 in per ASTM F1554.

inputs.label: Anchor Bolt Diameter Numeric Value
inputs.description: Numeric value of the anchor bolt diameter

inputs.label: Anchor Bolt Grade / Material
inputs.description: Anchor bolt material grade and strength class
options: ASTM F1554 Gr. 36 (Fy=250 MPa, Fu=400 MPa)
ASTM F1554 Gr. 55 (Fy=380 MPa, Fu=520 MPa)
ASTM F1554 Gr. 105 (Fy=720 MPa, Fu=860 MPa)
ASTM A307 Gr. A (Fu=414 MPa, general-purpose)
ASTM A354 Gr. BC (Fy=720-910 MPa, Fu=860-1034 MPa)
ASTM A354 Gr. BD (Fy=1034 MPa, Fu=1034 MPa)
ASTM A193 Gr. B7 (Fy=720 MPa, Fu=860 MPa, high-temp)

inputs.label: Anchor Bolt Yield Strength (F_y,ab)
inputs.description: Nominal yield strength of the anchor bolt material

inputs.label: Anchor Bolt Tensile Strength (F_u,ab)
inputs.description: Nominal ultimate tensile strength of the anchor bolt material

inputs.label: Anchor Bolt Effective Embedment Depth (h_ef)
inputs.description: Effective embedment depth of the anchor bolt measured from the concrete surface to the end of the anchor (or to the top of the anchor head/hook).

inputs.label: Number of Anchor Bolts (n)
inputs.description: Total number of anchor bolts in the base plate connection
options: 12 bolts (very heavy column or 2× pair pattern)

inputs.label: Number of Anchor Bolts (numeric value)
inputs.description: Numeric value of anchor bolt count

inputs.label: Bolt Gage Distance (X-direction, g_x)
inputs.description: Transverse spacing between anchor bolts (center-to-center) in the direction parallel to the column flange (X-direction)

inputs.label: Bolt Gage Distance (Y-direction, g_y)
inputs.description: Longitudinal spacing between anchor bolts (center-to-center) in the direction parallel to the column web (Y-direction)

inputs.label: Edge Distance — X-direction (c_a1)
inputs.description: Distance from the center of the anchor bolt to the edge of the concrete in the X-direction (primary edge for breakout). For ACI 318, the minimum edge distance controls.

inputs.label: Edge Distance — Y-direction (c_a2)
inputs.description: Distance from the center of the anchor bolt to the edge of the concrete in the Y-direction

inputs.label: Minimum Edge Distance (c_a,min)
inputs.description: Minimum edge distance from bolt center to any concrete edge. ACI 318-17.3.1: ca_min >= 1.5 × d_b for anchor bolts (increases with bolt size). EN 1992-4: c_min = 6 × d_nom for chemical anchors, 4 × d_nom for headed studs.

inputs.label: Anchor Bolt Hole Clearance (c_clear)
inputs.description: Clearance between anchor bolt diameter and base plate hole (typical: 6 mm for d <= 30 mm, 10 mm for d > 30 mm per AISC DG1)

inputs.label: Applied Axial Compression Load (P_u)
inputs.description: Factored axial compressive load on the column base (positive for compression). Use zero if only tension exists.

inputs.label: Applied Axial Tension (T_u — uplift)
inputs.description: Factored axial tension load on the column base / anchor bolts (positive for tension, uplift)

inputs.label: Applied Shear Force — X-direction (V_ux)
inputs.description: Factored shear force at the base in the X-direction (parallel to column flange)

inputs.label: Applied Shear Force — Y-direction (V_uy)
inputs.description: Factored shear force at the base in the Y-direction (parallel to column web)

inputs.label: Applied Strong-Axis Moment (M_u,xx)
inputs.description: Factored strong-axis bending moment at the column base (about the X-X axis, creating tension in the flanges)

inputs.label: Applied Weak-Axis Moment (M_u,yy)
inputs.description: Factored weak-axis bending moment at the column base (about the Y-Y axis)

inputs.label: Shear Friction Coefficient (mu)
inputs.description: Coefficient of friction between base plate and grout/concrete per AISC DG1 / ACI 318. 0.55 for grouted base plate on concrete (steel on grout).

inputs.label: Threads Included in Shear Plane?
inputs.description: Whether the threads of the anchor bolt are intercepted by the shear plane. Affects nominal shear strength per AISC 360 J3.6 / ACI 318 17.5.1.2.
options: Yes — threads included in shear plane (reduce shear capacity)
No — threads excluded from shear plane (full shank area in shear)

inputs.label: Supplementary Reinforcement Provided?
inputs.description: Whether supplementary reinforcement (hairpin ties, edge rebar, or anchor reinforcement) is provided to engage the concrete breakout cone per ACI 318 17.4.2.1 / EN 1992-4 §9.2.1.
options: Yes — anchor reinforcement designed to carry full tension (psi_reinf_N = 0.75)

inputs.label: Seismic Design Category (SDC per ASCE 7)
inputs.description: Seismic design category per ASCE 7 / EN 1998-1. Affects ductility requirements for anchor bolts (ACI 318 17.2.3).
options: Non-seismic — SDC A (no special anchor ductility)
Moderate seismicity — SDC C (ductile anchor required)
High seismicity — SDC D (ductile + anchor reinforcement required)
Very high seismicity — SDC E/F (full ductility design required)

inputs.label: Concrete Crack Condition
inputs.description: Whether the concrete is assumed cracked at the anchor location per ACI 318 17.2.4. Cracked concrete reduces breakout and pullout strength.
options: Cracked concrete (psi_c = 1.0 for breakout, default condition)

inputs.label: Seismic Overstrength Factor (omega_0)
inputs.description: Seismic overstrength factor for anchor design per ASCE 7-16 §12.4.3.2. Typically omega_0 = 2.0 for cantilever column systems, omega_0 = 2.5 for other systems.

inputs.label: Anchor Category (EN 1992-4)
inputs.description: Anchor category per EN 1992-4 §2.1.2. Determines which failure modes are applicable and partial factors required.
options: Category 1 — Low risk (static loads only, redundant)
Category 3 — High risk (seismic, cracked concrete, dynamic)

inputs.label: Concrete Partial Factor (gamma_c for concrete breakout)
inputs.description: Partial factor for concrete-related failure modes per EN 1992-4 Table 3.1. Recommended 1.5 for Category 2 (moderate). Check National Annex.

inputs.label: Steel Partial Factor (gamma_s for anchor steel)
inputs.description: Partial factor for steel failure modes of anchor per EN 1992-4 Table 3.1. Recommended 1.5 for Category 2 (moderate). Check National Annex.

inputs.label: Reduction Factor — Anchor Steel (phi_factor)
inputs.description: Strength reduction factor for anchor steel tension/shear per ACI 318 17.3.3. For ductile steel in tension: phi = 0.75. For shear: phi = 0.65 (if threads in shear plane) or 0.70 (if threads excluded).

inputs.label: Reduction Factor — Concrete Failure Modes
inputs.description: Strength reduction factor for concrete-related failure modes per ACI 318 17.3.3. For concrete breakout/pullout: phi = 0.70 (Condition A — supplementary reinforcement provided) or phi = 0.65 (Condition B — no supplementary reinforcement).

formulas.label: Base Plate Area (A_1 = B × N)
formulas.description: Gross area of the base plate. A_1 = B × N (width × depth).

formulas.label: Maximum Supported Concrete Area (A_2)
formulas.description: Maximum area of the concrete support that is geometrically similar to and concentric with A_1. For ACI 318, A_2 is bounded by the foundation plan dimensions.
formulas.expression: A_2 = Min(concrete_foundation_plan_dimensions_mm * concrete_foundation_depth_dimension_mm, 4 * A_1, (concrete_foundation_plan_dimensions_mm / base_plate_width_mm) * base_plate_width_mm * (concrete_foundation_depth_dimension_mm / base_plate_depth_mm) * base_plate_depth_mm)

formulas.label: Maximum Concrete Bearing Stress (AISC DG1 / ACI 318)
formulas.description: Maximum allowable concrete bearing stress under the base plate. ACI 318-22 Eq. 22.4.2.2: phi × 0.85 × f'_c × sqrt(A_2 / A_1), with sqrt(A_2/A_1) <= 2. For ACI 318-19: phi × 0.85 × f'_c × min(sqrt(A_2/A_1), 2).

formulas.label: Maximum Bearing Stress — Grout Enhanced
formulas.description: Maximum bearing stress considering grout strength. The lesser of (1) grout bearing capacity and (2) concrete bearing capacity per AISC DG1 §3.1.

formulas.label: Total Factored Axial Demand at Base (Compression)
formulas.description: Total factored compression at base considering axial load plus moment-induced compression on the bearing zone. The maximum bearing pressure occurs at the toe of the base plate under combined axial + moment.

formulas.label: Actual Maximum Bearing Stress Under Base Plate
formulas.description: Maximum bearing stress under combined axial compression and moment (tripartite linear distribution per AISC DG1). Based on rectangular bearing area: f = P/A + M*c/I.
formulas.expression: f_bearing_actual_max = (P_u * 1000) / A_1 + (M_u_xx * 1e6 * base_plate_depth_mm / 2) / (base_plate_width_mm * Power(base_plate_depth_mm, 3) / 12) + (M_u_yy * 1e6 * base_plate_width_mm / 2) / (Power(base_plate_width_mm, 3) * base_plate_depth_mm / 12)

formulas.label: Bearing Stress Utilization Ratio
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for concrete bearing. UC_bearing = f_bearing_actual_max / f_bearing_max_g. If <= 1.0, bearing stress is adequate.

formulas.label: Cantilever Distance for Base Plate Bending (m)
formulas.description: Critical cantilever distance from column face to plate edge for bending determination per AISC DG1 Eq. 3.1-1: m = (N - 0.95 × d_c) / 2. Also compute n = (B - 0.8 × bf_c) / 2, and lambda_n' = sqrt(d_c × bf_c) / 4.

formulas.label: Cantilever Distance for Base Plate Bending (n)
formulas.description: Cantilever distance perpendicular to m direction.

formulas.label: Cantilever Distance for Base Plate Bending (lambda_prime_n)
formulas.description: Geometric parameter for base plate bending from AISC DG1 Eq. 3.1-2: lambda_n' = sqrt(d_c × bf_c) / 4. Only applicable for I-sections under compression.

formulas.label: Critical Cantilever Length (l_max)
formulas.description: The governing cantilever distance for base plate thickness design (the largest of m, n, and lambda_n'). AISC DG1 §3.1: t_p,req = l_max × sqrt(2 × f_p / (0.90 × F_y)).

formulas.label: Required Base Plate Thickness (AISC DG1 Eq. 3.1.4)
formulas.description: Minimum required base plate thickness based on yield line cantilever bending. AISC DG1 Eq. 3.1-4: t_p,req = l_max × sqrt(2 × f_pu / (phi × F_y)), where phi = 0.9, f_pu = f_bearing_actual_max, F_y = base_plate_fy_mpa.

formulas.label: Base Plate Thickness Utilization
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base plate thickness. UC = t_p_required / t_p. If <= 1.0, the provided plate thickness is adequate.

formulas.label: Anchor Bolt Net Tensile Area (A_se)
formulas.description: Net tensile stress area of the anchor bolt per AISC DG1 Table 3.2 / ACI 318 17.5.1.2. Based on nominal bolt diameter and thread pitch. Approximated as: A_se = 0.75 × A_gross for threaded portion.

formulas.label: Anchor Bolt Tension Demand per Bolt
formulas.description: Maximum tensile force per anchor bolt from combined axial tension and overturning moment. Assumes rigid base plate with triangular distribution of bolt forces.

formulas.label: Anchor Bolt Shear Demand per Bolt
formulas.description: Maximum shear force per anchor bolt from combined V_ux and V_uy. V_resultant = sqrt(V_ux² + V_uy²), distributed equally among bolts.

formulas.label: Anchor Bolt Steel Strength in Tension (N_sa)
formulas.description: Nominal steel strength of an anchor in tension per ACI 318 17.5.1.2: N_sa = A_se × f_uta. futa is the specified tensile strength. Per ASTM F1554: futa = F_u,ab.

formulas.label: Design Steel Strength in Tension
formulas.description: Design steel strength in tension: phi_N_sa = phi_factor_anchor_steel × N_sa.

formulas.label: Concrete Breakout Strength in Tension — Single Anchor
formulas.description: Single anchor concrete breakout strength per ACI 318 17.6.2.0.1: N_b = k_c × lambda_a × sqrt(f'_c) × (h_ef)^1.5. For cast-in headed stud: k_c = 10 (cracked) or 12.5 (uncracked). For post-installed: per ETA.
formulas.expression: N_sb = Iif(cracked_concrete_condition == "Uncracked concrete (psi_c = 1.25 for breakout)", 12.5, 10) * 1.0 * Sqrt(concrete_compressive_strength_mpa) * Power(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm / 25.4, 1.5) * 1000

formulas.label: Projected Concrete Breakout Area (A_Nc)
formulas.description: Actual projected concrete failure area of the anchor group in tension per ACI 318 17.6.2.1. Based on spacing, edge distances, and 1.5 × hef failure cone projection.
formulas.expression: A_Nc = Min(bolt_gage_distance_x_mm + 3 * Min(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm, 1.5 * anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm), bolt_edge_distance_x_mm + 3 * Min(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm, 1.5 * anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm)) * Min(bolt_gage_distance_y_mm + 3 * Min(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm, 1.5 * anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm), bolt_edge_distance_y_mm + 3 * Min(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm, 1.5 * anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm))

formulas.label: Projected Breakout Area — Single Anchor (A_Nc0)
formulas.description: Projected area of a single anchor in tension with full 1.5 × hef cone (no edge effect) per ACI 318 17.6.2.1. A_Nc0 = 9 × h_ef².

formulas.label: Eccentricity Factor for Tension (psi_ec,N)
formulas.description: Modification factor for eccentric tensile loading per ACI 318 17.6.2.4.1: psi_ec,N = 1.0 / (1 + 2 × e_N' / (3 × h_ef)). Conservative: assume e_N' = 0 for concentric anchor group.

formulas.label: Edge Effect Factor for Tension (psi_ed,N)
formulas.description: Modification factor for edge distance per ACI 318 17.6.2.5: If ca_min >= 1.5 × hef, psi_ed,N = 1.0. If ca_min < 1.5 × hef, psi_ed,N = 0.7 + 0.3 × ca_min / (1.5 × hef).

formulas.label: Crack Condition Factor for Tension (psi_c,N)
formulas.description: Modification factor for concrete crack condition per ACI 318 17.6.2.6: psi_c,N = 1.0 for cracked concrete, 1.25 for uncracked concrete.

formulas.label: Design Concrete Breakout Strength in Tension (Group)
formulas.description: Nominal concrete breakout strength in tension for the anchor group per ACI 318 17.6.2.0.1: N_cbg = (A_Nc / A_Nc0) × psi_ec,N × psi_ed,N × psi_c,N × psi_reinf,N × N_sb. phi_N_cbg = phi_concrete × N_cbg.
formulas.expression: phi_N_cbg = phi_factor_concrete * (A_Nc / A_Nc0) * psi_ec_N * psi_ed_N * psi_c_N * 1.0 * N_sb

formulas.label: Concrete Pullout Strength in Tension (N_pn)
formulas.description: Nominal pullout strength of a single anchor in tension per ACI 318 17.7.1.2: N_pn = psi_c,P × N_p. For headed stud: N_p = 8 × A_brg × f'_c. A_brg is the bearing area of the anchor head. For hooked bolt: N_p = 0.9 × f'_c × e_h × d_b.
formulas.expression: N_pn = Iif(cracked_concrete_condition == "Uncracked concrete (psi_c = 1.25 for breakout)", 1.4, 1.0) * 8.0 * Power(anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm, 2) * PI / 4 * concrete_compressive_strength_mpa

formulas.label: Design Pullout Strength in Tension
formulas.description: Design pullout strength of anchor: phi_N_pn = phi_factor_concrete × N_pn.

formulas.label: Concrete Side-Face Blowout Strength (N_sb)
formulas.description: Nominal side-face blowout strength per ACI 318 17.7.3.1: N_sb = 160 × ca × sqrt(A_brg) × lambda_a × sqrt(f'_c). Applicable when ca1 > 0.4 × h_ef (deep embedment with edge distance).
formulas.expression: N_sb_side = Iif(bolt_edge_distance_min_mm > 0.4 * anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm, 160 * bolt_edge_distance_min_mm * Sqrt(Power(anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm, 2) * PI / 4) * 1.0 * Sqrt(concrete_compressive_strength_mpa), 0)

formulas.label: Design Side-Face Blowout Strength
formulas.description: Design side-face blowout strength: phi_N_sb = phi_factor_concrete × N_sb_side (N_sb_side for the anchor with the largest tension). For group: phi_N_sb_group = (1 + s/(6×ca)) × phi_N_sb.

formulas.label: Governing Design Tension Resistance per Bolt
formulas.description: Minimum of all design tension resistances for the anchor bolt per ACI 318 17.4.1: N_design = min(phi_N_sa, phi_N_cbg / n_ab, phi_N_pn, phi_N_sb).
formulas.expression: N_governing_per_bolt = Min(phi_N_sa, phi_N_cbg / Max(number_of_anchor_bolts_val, 1), phi_N_pn, phi_N_sb)

formulas.label: Anchor Bolt Tension Utilization
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for anchor bolt in tension: UC = T_u_per_bolt / N_governing_per_bolt. Includes steel strength, concrete breakout, pullout, and side-face blowout checks.

formulas.label: Anchor Bolt Steel Strength in Shear (V_sa)
formulas.description: Nominal steel strength of an anchor in shear per ACI 318 17.5.1.2: V_sa = 0.6 × A_se × f_uta (threads included) or V_sa = 0.6 × A_gross × f_uta (threads excluded).
formulas.expression: V_nsa = Iif(threads_included_in_shear_plane == "Yes — threads included in shear plane (reduce shear capacity)", 0.6 * A_se_N * anchor_bolt_fu_mpa, 0.6 * Power(anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm, 2) * PI / 4 * anchor_bolt_fu_mpa)

formulas.label: Design Steel Strength in Shear
formulas.description: Design steel strength in shear: phi_V_sa = phi_factor_anchor_steel × V_nsa. For shear, phi_factor_anchor_steel per ACI 318 17.3.3: 0.60 (ductile, condition A) or 0.65 (brittle). Use 0.60 conservative for threads included.
formulas.expression: phi_V_sa = Iif(threads_included_in_shear_plane == "Yes — threads included in shear plane (reduce shear capacity)", 0.60, 0.65) * V_nsa

formulas.label: Concrete Breakout Strength in Shear — Single Anchor
formulas.description: Concrete breakout strength of a single anchor in shear per ACI 318 17.7.2.0.1: V_b = (l_e / d_b)^0.2 × lambda_a × sqrt(f'_c) × (ca1)^1.5. For cast-in headed stud with d_b <= 25 mm and l_e / d_b >= 8: base equation.
formulas.expression: V_cbg = (Min(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm, 8 * anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm) / anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm >= 8, 8 / Sqrt(anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm / 25.4), 0.2) * 1.0 * Sqrt(concrete_compressive_strength_mpa) * Power(bolt_edge_distance_min_mm, 1.5) * 7.0 * Power(anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm / 25.4, 0.2)

formulas.label: Design Concrete Breakout Strength in Shear (Group)
formulas.description: Design concrete breakout strength in shear for the anchor group per ACI 318 17.7.2.
formulas.expression: phi_V_cbg = phi_factor_concrete * V_cbg * Min(number_of_anchor_bolts_val * bolt_edge_distance_min_mm / (3 * Min(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm, 1.5 * anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm)), 1.0)

formulas.label: Concrete Pryout Strength in Shear
formulas.description: Concrete pryout strength per ACI 318 17.7.3: V_cp = k_cp × N_cbg. k_cp = 2.0 for anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm >= 280mm, k_cp = 1.0 for h_ef < 280mm.
formulas.expression: V_cpg = Iif(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm >= 280, 2.0, 1.0) * (A_Nc / A_Nc0) * psi_ec_N * psi_ed_N * psi_c_N * 1.0 * N_sb

formulas.label: Design Concrete Pryout Strength
formulas.description: Design concrete pryout strength: phi_V_cpg = phi_factor_concrete × V_cpg.

formulas.label: Governing Design Shear Resistance per Bolt
formulas.description: Minimum of all design shear resistances for the anchor bolt: V_design = min(phi_V_sa, phi_V_cbg / n_ab, phi_V_cpg / n_ab).
formulas.expression: V_governing_per_bolt = Min(phi_V_sa, phi_V_cbg / Max(number_of_anchor_bolts_val, 1), phi_V_cpg / Max(number_of_anchor_bolts_val, 1))

formulas.label: Anchor Bolt Shear Utilization
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for anchor bolt in shear: UC = V_u_per_bolt / V_governing_per_bolt.

formulas.label: Anchor Combined Tension + Shear Interaction
formulas.description: Combined tension and shear interaction per ACI 318 17.5.3 / AISC 360 Eq. J3-3a and J3-3b: If V_u / phi_V_n <= 0.2, interaction = T_u / phi_T_n + V_u / phi_V_n <= 1.0. If V_u / phi_V_n > 0.2, interaction = T_u / phi_T_n + 1.67 × V_u / phi_V_n <= 1.0.
formulas.expression: UC_combined_TS = Iif(UC_anchor_shear <= 0.2, UC_anchor_tension + UC_anchor_shear, UC_anchor_tension + 1.67 * UC_anchor_shear)

formulas.label: Shear Friction Resistance (Base Plate to Grout)
formulas.description: Shear resistance by friction between base plate and grout per AISC DG1 §3.2: V_friction = mu × P_u. Based on shear friction coefficient and factored axial compression. Must be used in conjunction with anchor shear capacity.

formulas.label: Shear Friction Adequacy Check
formulas.description: Checks whether shear friction alone can resist the applied shear (base plate sliding check). If V_friction_resistance >= total V_u, anchors are not required to resist shear.
formulas.expression: V_friction_check = Iif(V_friction_resistance >= Sqrt(Power(V_ux * 1000, 2) + Power(V_uy * 1000, 2)), "PASS — friction resistance adequate, no anchor shear demand", Iif(V_friction_resistance >= 0.5 * Sqrt(Power(V_ux * 1000, 2) + Power(V_uy * 1000, 2)), "MODERATE — friction reduces but does not eliminate anchor shear demand", "FAIL — friction resistance insufficient, anchors must resist full shear"))

formulas.label: Governing Utilization Ratio (All Checks)
formulas.description: Overall governing demand-capacity ratio considering bearing, plate bending, anchor tension, anchor shear, and combined interaction. UC_governing = max(UC_bearing, UC_plate_thickness, UC_anchor_tension, UC_anchor_shear, UC_combined_TS).
formulas.expression: UC_governing = Max(UC_bearing, UC_plate_thickness, UC_anchor_tension, UC_anchor_shear, UC_combined_TS)

formulas.label: Governing Mode Summary
formulas.description: Identifies the governing failure mode based on the highest utilization ratio among all design checks
formulas.expression: governing_mode_summary = Iif(UC_governing == UC_bearing, "Concrete bearing stress under base plate governs", Iif(UC_governing == UC_plate_thickness, "Base plate bending / thickness governs", Iif(UC_governing == UC_anchor_tension, "Anchor bolt tension resistance governs (steel / breakout / pullout)", Iif(UC_governing == UC_anchor_shear, "Anchor bolt shear resistance governs (steel / breakout / pryout)", "Anchor bolt combined tension+shear interaction governs"))))

formulas.label: Minimum Edge Distance Check (ACI 318 / EN 1992-4)
formulas.description: Verification that the anchor bolt edge distance meets minimum code requirements per ACI 318 17.3.1: ca_min >= 1.5 × d_b for anchor bolts in tension. EN 1992-4: c >= 4 × d_nom (headed) or 6 × d_nom (chemical).
formulas.expression: min_edge_distance_check = Iif(bolt_edge_distance_min_mm >= 1.5 * anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm, "PASS — edge distance meets ACI 318 minimum requirements", "FAIL — insufficient edge distance per ACI 318 17.3.1.1. Edge distance must be at least 1.5 × d_b.")

formulas.label: Minimum Embedment Depth Check
formulas.description: Verification that the anchor bolt embedment depth meets minimum requirements per ACI 318 17.3.1.3: hef >= 12 × d_b for cast-in headed stud anchors in tension.
formulas.expression: min_embedment_check = Iif(anchor_bolt_embedment_depth_mm >= 12 * anchor_bolt_diameter_val_mm, "PASS — embedment depth meets ACI 318 minimum of 12 × d_b", "FAIL — insufficient embedment depth per ACI 318 17.3.1.3. Minimum: 12 × d_b for cast-in headed stud anchors.")

outputs.label: Governing Utilization Ratio
outputs.description: Overall governing demand-capacity ratio for the base plate and anchor bolt connection

outputs.label: Governing Failure Mode
outputs.description: Identifies the controlling failure mode based on highest utilization

outputs.label: Bearing Stress Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for concrete bearing stress under the base plate

outputs.label: Base Plate Thickness Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base plate bending (required vs. provided thickness)

outputs.label: Required Base Plate Thickness
outputs.description: Minimum required base plate thickness per AISC DG1 yield line method

outputs.label: Critical Cantilever Length
outputs.description: Governing cantilever distance for base plate thickness design

outputs.label: Actual Maximum Bearing Stress
outputs.description: Maximum bearing stress under combined axial + moment at the base plate toe

outputs.label: Allowable Bearing Stress
outputs.description: Maximum allowable concrete bearing stress (grout-enhanced)

outputs.label: Anchor Bolt Tension Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for anchor bolt in tension (steel + breakout + pullout + blowout)

outputs.label: Anchor Bolt Shear Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for anchor bolt in shear (steel + breakout + pryout)

outputs.label: Combined Tension+Shear Interaction Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio per ACI 318 17.5.3 / AISC J3.7 interaction equation

outputs.label: Tension Demand per Bolt
outputs.description: Maximum tensile force per anchor bolt from uplift + overturning moment

outputs.label: Shear Demand per Bolt
outputs.description: Maximum shear force per anchor bolt from resultant V_ux + V_uy

outputs.label: Governing Tension Resistance per Bolt
outputs.description: Minimum of steel, breakout, pullout, and side-face blowout tension resistances

outputs.label: Governing Shear Resistance per Bolt
outputs.description: Minimum of steel, concrete breakout, and pryout shear resistances

outputs.label: Design Steel Strength — Tension
outputs.description: Design steel strength of anchor in tension

outputs.label: Design Breakout Strength — Tension (Group)
outputs.description: Design concrete breakout strength in tension for anchor group

outputs.label: Design Steel Strength — Shear
outputs.description: Design steel strength of anchor in shear

outputs.label: Design Breakout Strength — Shear (Group)
outputs.description: Design concrete breakout strength in shear for anchor group

outputs.label: Shear Friction Adequacy
outputs.description: Assessment of whether shear friction alone is adequate or anchors must resist shear

outputs.label: Edge Distance Check
outputs.description: Verification of minimum edge distance per ACI 318 / EN 1992-4

outputs.label: Minimum Embedment Check
outputs.description: Verification of minimum embedment depth per ACI 318 / EN 1992-4

rules.message: Base plate width (N) must extend at least 25 mm beyond each side of the column flange (N >= bf_c + 50 mm) per AISC DG1 §3.1.
Base plate depth (B) must extend at least 25 mm beyond each side of the column (B >= d_c + 50 mm) per AISC DG1 §3.1.
Minimum embedment depth is 4 × d_b per ACI 318 17.3.1.3 for headed stud anchors (12 × d_b for tension). Current embedment is insufficient.
Minimum edge distance per ACI 318 17.3.1.1: edge distance must be at least 1.5 × d_b for anchor bolts. Current edge distance is insufficient.
Absolute minimum edge distance for any anchor bolt is 25 mm (1 inch) per AISC DG1 Table 3.3 and EN 1992-4 §4.2.
Minimum number of anchor bolts for a column base plate is 2. Small moment connections typically use 4 bolts.
Anchor bolt yield strength cannot exceed ultimate tensile strength (Fy > Fu). Check bolt grade specification.
Grout compressive strength should be at least 0.5 × f'_c. Weak grout reduces effective bearing capacity. Consider using higher-strength non-shrink grout (min 50 MPa recommended).

rules.condition: applied_axial_compression_kN <= 0 AND applied_axial_tension_kN <= 0 AND applied_shear_y_kN <= 0 AND applied_moment_strong_axis_kNm <= 0
rules.message: At least one applied load must be entered. Enter the factored loads on the column base.

rules.condition: seismic_design_category CONTAINS "High seismicity" OR seismic_design_category CONTAINS "Very high seismicity"
rules.message: High/very high seismicity (SDC D/E/F) selected. ACI 318 17.2.3.5 requires ductile anchor design with: (1) steel failure governs over concrete failure, (2) minimum edge distance >= 2 × d_b, (3) anchor reinforcement to resist at least 0.5 × N_u or V_u. Verify compliance.
Minimum anchor bolt diameter for structural column base connections is M12 (12 mm) or 1/2 inch. Smaller bolts are not suitable for structural connections.
Bolt gage distance in X-direction exceeds base plate width. Verify bolt layout dimensions.

smart_warnings.message: CONNECTION OVERSTRESSED: Governing utilization UC = [UC_governing] exceeds 1.0. The base plate/anchor bolt connection is insufficient. Actions: (1) Increase base plate size (N, B, or t_p), (2) Use larger or more anchor bolts, (3) Increase embedment depth, (4) Use higher-strength bolt grade, (5) Increase concrete strength or foundation size, (6) Provide supplementary reinforcement.
CONNECTION HIGHLY STRESSED: Governing UC = [UC_governing]. Consider increasing plate thickness or bolt size for economical design. Verify anchor installation quality and NDT inspection requirements.

Anchor steel strength governs tension. This is preferred per ACI 318 17.2.3.5 (ductile steel failure). Ensure bolts have adequate ductility (elongation >= 14% for seismic). Verify bolt material certification.

smart_warnings.condition: N_governing_per_bolt == phi_N_cbg / Max(number_of_anchor_bolts_val, 1) AND phi_N_cbg / Max(number_of_anchor_bolts_val, 1) < 0.5 * phi_N_sa
smart_warnings.message: Concrete breakout governs tension at less than 50% of steel strength. This is a brittle failure mode per ACI 318 17.2.3.5. For seismic applications, this is not permitted. Provide supplementary reinforcement (hairpin ties) to ensure ductile failure mode. Per ACI 318 17.4.2.1: reinforcement designed to carry tension [N_reinf >= T_u] may increase phi factor.

smart_warnings.condition: total_V_u > 0.5 * V_friction_resistance AND V_friction_resistance < Sqrt(Power(V_ux * 1000, 2) + Power(V_uy * 1000, 2))
smart_warnings.message: Shear friction provides some but not full resistance. Anchor bolts must be designed for shear in addition to tension. Verify that combined tension + shear interaction (AISC J3.7 / ACI 318 17.5.3) is satisfied.

smart_warnings.condition: seismic_design_category CONTAINS "High seismicity" OR seismic_design_category CONTAINS "Very high seismicity"
smart_warnings.message: High seismicity (SDC D+): Anchor ductility requirements per ACI 318 17.2.3.5: (1) Steel failure must govern — required: phi_N_sa < phi_N_cbg (breakout capacity reduced by 0.75 factor). (2) Minimum anchor spacing >= 8 × d_b. (3) Anchor reinforcement to carry at least 0.5 × N_u (tension) around anchor group. (4) Oversized holes must be compensated with hardened washers or plate washers welded to base plate.

Base plate thickness > 75 mm is unusually thick. Verify economy: (1) Consider adding stiffeners between column flanges to reduce required plate thickness, (2) Consider using higher-strength steel for base plate (e.g., A572 Gr. 50 to A572 Gr. 65), (3) Verify that base plate bending governs and not bearing or bolt capacity. (4) Check if a two-plate built-up base (with doubler) is more economical.

combined_uncertainty.notes: Combined standard uncertainty estimated at 10.2% based on GUM linear approximation. Dominant contributions: concrete compressive strength (±3 MPa, ~6% sensitivity on breakout), embedment depth (±10 mm, ~5.5% sensitivity on breakout capacity), and bolt tensile strength (±40 MPa, ~4% sensitivity on steel strength). A combined UC of 1.02 or less under best-estimate inputs could result in actual UC exceeding 1.0 at 95% confidence. Consider applying a design margin of 8–10% for critical connections, or perform site-specific anchor pull-out testing to reduce uncertainty.

fmea.failureMode: Concrete bearing failure under base plate
fmea.effect: Crushing of concrete beneath base plate. Settlement and rotation of column base. Loss of vertical alignment.
fmea.control_measure: AISC DG1 §3.1 / ACI 318 22.4.2 bearing check. Verify A_2 / A_1 ratio <= 2 for conservative design. Use non-shrink grout with f_g >= 0.5 × f'_c.
fmea.description: Governs when UC_bearing > UC_plate and UC_bearing > UC_anchor checks. Ensure UC_bearing <= 1.0.

fmea.failureMode: Base plate yielding in bending (ductile flexural failure)
fmea.effect: Excessive plate deformation under column load. Flange-induced bending (m or n cantilever). Permanent set of plate.
fmea.control_measure: AISC DG1 §3.1 Eq. 3.1-4. Verify t_p >= t_p,required based on l_max and bearing stress. Use yield line theory (Thornton method).
fmea.description: Plate thickness check in UC_plate_thickness. Ensure t_p >= t_p,required.

fmea.effect: Bolt necking and rupture at threaded section. Sudden loss of tension capacity. Uplift of base plate.
fmea.control_measure: ACI 318 17.5.1 / AISC J3.6. Verify A_se × f_uta × phi >= T_u. Check bolt elongation >= 14% for seismic ductility. Use ASTM F1554 Gr. 55 or Gr. 105 for improved ductility.
fmea.description: Steel tension check through phi_N_sa vs. T_u_per_bolt. Preferred failure mode per ACI 318 for seismic.

fmea.failureMode: Concrete breakout in tension (brittle cone failure)
fmea.effect: Cone-shaped concrete fracture emanating from the anchor head. Complete loss of tension anchorage. Brittle failure.
fmea.control_measure: ACI 318 17.6.2 (CCD method). Verify A_Nc/A_Nc0 × psi_ec,N × psi_ed,N × psi_c,N × N_b >= T_u / phi. Provide supplementary reinforcement (hairpin ties engaging breakout cone). Ensure edge distance >= 1.5 × h_ef or reduce cone projection.
fmea.description: Breakout check in phi_N_cbg. Most critical concrete failure mode for shallow-embedment anchors.

fmea.failureMode: Anchor pullout (headed stud bearing failure in concrete)
fmea.effect: Anchor head pulls through concrete. Bearing failure of concrete under anchor head. Loss of tension capacity.
fmea.control_measure: ACI 318 17.7.1. Verify A_brg × f'_c × psi_c,P × phi >= T_u. Use headed studs with minimum head diameter >= 2.5 × d_b. Hooked bolts: verify e_h >= 3 × d_b.
fmea.description: Pullout check in phi_N_pn. Critical for smooth shaft anchors with small head.

fmea.effect: Lateral concrete splitting failure at side face near anchor head. Applicable for deep embedment ca1 > 0.4 × h_ef.
fmea.control_measure: ACI 318 17.7.3. Verify ca1 >= 0.4 × h_ef to avoid blowout check requirement. Provide edge reinforcement (longitudinal rebar along edge face). Reduce edge distance or use smaller headed anchors.
fmea.description: Side-face blowout in phi_N_sb. Prevents splitting of thin concrete cover.

fmea.failureMode: Anchor bolt shear rupture (steel or concrete breakout)
fmea.effect: Shear failure of bolt at the concrete surface or concrete breakout edge failure. Lateral slip of base plate.
fmea.control_measure: ACI 318 17.5.1 (steel shear) + 17.7.2 (breakout shear) + 17.7.3 (pryout). Exclude threads from shear plane when possible. Provide shear lug for large shear loads (> 30% of compression load).
fmea.description: Shear check in UC_anchor_shear. Combined with tension in UC_combined_TS.

fmea.failureMode: Base plate slip / sliding under lateral load
fmea.effect: Lateral displacement of column base. Friction or anchor shear inadequate. Loss of lateral stability.
fmea.control_measure: AISC DG1 §3.2. Verify shear friction: V_friction = mu × P_u >= V_u. If not, anchors must resist full shear. Consider shear lug for large shear loads (welded plate or channel embedded in concrete).
fmea.description: Shear friction check determines anchor shear demand. Insufficient friction increases bolt demand.

required_entries: Design code and edition (AISC DG1 / ACI 318-19/22 / EN 1992-4) with applicable National Annex
Column section profile, grade, material certificate (EN 10204 3.1 or ASTM mill cert)
Base plate material grade and thickness — steel mill certificate reference
Anchor bolt specification: grade, diameter, lot number, manufacturer certification (ASTM F1554 or ISO 898)
Concrete compressive strength test results (cylinder tests at 28 days per ASTM C39 / EN 12390-3)
Grout compressive strength test results (cube or cylinder tests, min 7-day and 28-day)
Anchor bolt installation inspection: embedment depth, edge distances, spacing, plumbness, and level (AISC Code of Standard Practice §7)
Anchor bolt tension test records (if specified in project specifications, e.g., AISC 360 §J9.1)
Seismic design category determination and anchor ductility verification (ACI 318 17.2.3 / ASCE 7)
Concrete condition at anchor location (cracked/uncracked) per design assumption vs. field observation
Supplementary reinforcement details: size, grade, placement, and cover
Base plate leveling and grouting procedure and inspection records
Deviations, non-conformances, and corrective actions during installation

helpers_required.name: lookup_anchor_bolt_properties
helpers_required.description: Map anchor bolt grade to yield strength, tensile strength, reduction factors, and recommended edge distances per ASTM / ISO

helpers_required.name: lookup_bolt_tensile_area
helpers_required.description: Compute net tensile area (A_se) from bolt diameter and thread pitch per ISO 898-1 / ASME B1.1 unified thread standard

helpers_required.name: compute_breakout_geometry
helpers_required.description: Compute A_Nc / A_Nc0 for arbitrary bolt group layout (n bolts, varying spacing and edge distances) using CCD method per ACI 318 17.6

helpers_required.name: compute_bolt_layout_forces
helpers_required.description: Compute bolt tension distribution under combined axial + biaxial moment using elastic bolt group method (linear strain distribution, rigid plate assumption)

helpers_required.name: check_seismic_anchor_ductility
helpers_required.description: Per ACI 318 17.2.3.5, verify that anchor steel strength governs over concrete breakout with appropriate overstrength factor. Checks ductility requirements for SDC C through F.

primary.title: Base Plate and Anchor Rod Design (2nd Edition)
primary.description: Primary US reference for column base plate design including bearing, bending, and anchor rod determination.

primary.title: Anchoring to Concrete
primary.description: Governing US standard for anchor bolt design including steel, concrete breakout, pullout, blowout, and combined loading.

primary.title: Design of fastenings for use in concrete
primary.description: Governing European standard for the design of anchor channels and headed fasteners in concrete.

secondary.title: Specification for Structural Steel Buildings — Design of Connections
secondary.description: AISC specification for anchor rod design including tensile strength, shear strength, and combined interaction.

secondary.title: Seismic Design Requirements for Building Structures
secondary.description: Seismic design requirements affecting anchor bolt ductility and overstrength design.

secondary.title: Standard Specification for Anchor Bolts, Steel, 36, 55, and 105 ksi Yield Strength
secondary.description: Standard specification for anchor bolts including material properties, strength grades, and dimensional requirements.

secondary.title: Execution of steel structures — Part 2: Technical requirements
secondary.description: Execution classes and fabrication/inspection requirements for steel connections including base plates.

reference_code.standards_organization: AISC (American Institute of Steel Construction) + ACI (American Concrete Institute) + CEN (European Committee for Standardization)
